---
title: "How it works"
description: "The core ideas behind OpenPipeline, and why it works the way it does."
order: 0
---

OpenPipeline is a set of modular, containerized workflows for single-cell and
spatial multi-omics, taking data from raw counts to a processed, annotated
object. A few design choices set it apart:

- **True modularity.** Modular not just in the source tree but at the
  *execution* level: every step is an independently built, containerized unit you
  can run, test, swap, or reorder on its own, and even declare as a dependency
  from another package's repository.
- **Standard multi-omics data object.** All modalities, RNA, protein, ATAC,
  spatial, and more, share one standard [MuData](mudata.qmd) object, so any
  component composes with any other.
- **Reproducible.** Each component pins its exact dependencies in a container,
  and every release is versioned, so the same run gives the same results today
  and years from now.
- **Scalable and portable.** Workflows are Nextflow pipelines, so the same run
  goes unchanged from a laptop to an HPC cluster or the cloud, parallelizing
  across samples.
- **Best practices, by default.** Brings software-engineering rigor to
  bioinformatics, and follows the single-cell community's analysis best
  practices.
- **Community-driven, team-maintained.** Open source and developed in the open,
  but not a release-once academic project: actively maintained for years by a
  dedicated team.

The pages below explain how those choices play out. Read them top to bottom for
the full picture, or jump to whichever answers your question.

```{=html}
<div class="concept-list">

  <a class="concept-card" href="ecosystem.html">
    <div class="ct">The OpenPipeline ecosystem</div>
    <p>A core package and a family of satellites that all share the same building blocks.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="modularity.html">
    <div class="ct">Components, workflows &amp; modularity</div>
    <p>Every step is a self-contained unit you can run, swap, or reorder on its own.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="mudata.html">
    <div class="ct">MuData as standard data format</div>
    <p>The shared data format that lets any component fit together with any other.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="viash.html">
    <div class="ct">The engine: Viash and Viash Hub</div>
    <p>How each script becomes a shareable, reproducible component.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="param-list.html">
    <div class="ct">Many samples at once: the param_list</div>
    <p>How a single run processes an entire cohort of samples at once.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="architecture.html">
    <div class="ct">Pipeline architecture</div>
    <p>How data flows from raw sequencing to an annotated result.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card blue" href="data-state.html">
    <div class="ct">Advanced: data states</div>
    <p>The pipeline seen through the changing shape of the data, a view for authors.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

</div>
```
